mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, providing efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is ideal for use cases involving semantic search, recommendation systems, and neural network applications. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient resources to handle large datasets and the complexity of managing configurations for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Qdrant CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Qdrant instances.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/qdrant/qdrant/releases/download/v1.17.0/qdrant-cli-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/qdrant

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/qdrant

```

> Download the Qdrant CLI binary.
> Make the Qdrant CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Qdrant Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Qdrant cluster in your managed Kubernetes environment with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create qdrant-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Qdrant cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials qdrant-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with Google Cloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Qdrant cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your Qdrant deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/k8s/qdrant-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/k8s/qdrant-monitoring.yaml

```

> This configures autoscaling for your Qdrant deployment.
> This sets up monitoring for your Qdrant instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will delete the Qdrant cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete qdrant-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the cluster without confirmation.

uninstall.title: Remove Qdrant CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Qdrant CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/qdrant

```

> This will delete the Qdrant CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and check the compatibility of your configurations.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n qdrant -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

gcloud container clusters upgrade qdrant-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --region=us-central1

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Drain all running pods.
> Upgrade the cluster to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n qdrant -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant server may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Qdrant pods using 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to start Qdrant'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Configuration file not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant configuration file may not be correctly mounted or is missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure the configuration file is present and correctly referenced in your deployment manifest.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms, improved API response times.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your indexing configurations to use the new algorithms.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced enhanced filtering capabilities for vector searches.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for older filtering syntax.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Revise your filtering queries to align with the new syntax.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in the 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Google Cloud account and the necessary permissions to create Kubernetes clusters.
